task.title: Revise main CodeWiki bootstrap skill
task.summary: Update the main `codewiki` skill so it owns bootstrap, status, onboarding, and loop routing without embedding compiler-specific workflow detail.

goal.outcome: Main `codewiki` skill is a lean package-safe entry point for bootstrap/status/router behavior, including sprint-aware routing for related executable work.
acceptance: `skills/codewiki/SKILL.md` describes when to use the main skill for bootstrap, status, onboarding, and selecting the next compiler/gateway mode.
Bootstrap/onboarding guidance is reachable through package-local skill references and does not rely on unpublished `.codewiki/**` links.
Main skill lists available commands/tools accurately enough for first contact, then routes detailed loop work to focused loop skills.
Main skill/router guidance states when related executable work should be tracked through sprint metadata and routes that decision to planning/tool-contract support.
non_goals: Do not author detailed compiler/gateway loop instructions in the main skill.
Do not change runtime bootstrap command behavior unless needed to keep prompt assets loading.
Do not hand-edit roadmap sprint metadata directly from the skill guidance.

delta.desired: Main skill is package-safe, concise, and optimized as the agents' CodeWiki entry point.
delta.current: Main skill mixes package surface, invariants, loop pointers, and broken package-local related-doc assumptions.
delta.closure: Bootstrap/status/router guidance is clear and tested without overloading loop behavior.
